title: Chaubardia heloisae
intro: Chaubardia heloisae is a plant species native to the tropical regions of Central and South America, classified under the family Apocynaceae and commonly known as heliotropium. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, providing relief from various health issues such as inflammation and respiratory problems. The bioactive constituents of Chaubardia heloisae include alkaloids, flavonoids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Chaubardia heloisae is often prepared as a decoction or an infusion, and is also used in the form of tinctures, capsules, and teas to utilize its health benefits.
